text.title: Lightweight Monitoring + Logging Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `Lightweight Monitoring + Logging` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: journald MUST have `SystemMaxUse` + `SystemKeepFree` set (e.g. 2G / 1G). Unbounded growth = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Default journald accumulates without bound; on a 40GB VPS this fills the disk within months.

rule.statement: Daily digest MUST emit exactly one line per service summarizing 24h: state + restarts + error count. Multi-line per service = REJECT.
rule.rationale: One scannable Telegram message vs a dashboard scroll; signal-density per pixel matters.

rule.statement: Per-incident alerts MUST fire only on threshold breach or service failure. Success spam = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Daily digest is the success channel; per-incident alert is the failure channel — never mix.

rule.statement: Every service log line MUST include a correlation id (request-id / job-id). Plain prose logs = REJECT for any service that handles user requests.
rule.rationale: Without correlation id, debugging a user incident means timestamp-grepping across 5 logs by hand.

rule.statement: Logs MUST stay on the VPS by default; shipping to third-party cloud (Datadog, Sentry logs) requires explicit opt-in + cost line. Default-ship = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Log-shipping bills scale with traffic; default-on is a budget surprise waiting to happen.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume cycles and produce false confidence.
